chapter_summary: Sarai, still childless, gives her servant Hagar to Abram to bear a child for her. When Hagar conceives, she despises Sarai; Sarai treats her harshly and Hagar flees. The Angel of the LORD finds Hagar at a spring in the wilderness, tells her to return, promises that her son Ishmael will be great, and she names God 'El Roi' — the God who sees. Hagar returns and Ishmael is born when Abram is 86.
main_lesson: When we try to fix God's plan our own way, things can get messy. But God still sees everyone — even people who feel forgotten — and He is always kind.

memory_verse.kjv: And she called the name of the LORD that spake unto her, Thou God seest me: for she said, Have I also here looked after him that seeth me?
memory_verse.little_bible: Hagar gave God a special name: 'You are the God who sees me.' Even when she was alone and afraid in the desert, God saw her and cared for her.

parent_guide: This is a difficult chapter — Sarai's scheme (using a slave as surrogate) reflects both ancient practice and human impatience with God. The beautiful theological core is Hagar's encounter: she is the first person in the Bible to name God, and she calls Him 'the God who sees me' (El Roi). For children, the Hagar story is the most important part: God sees and cares for people who feel invisible or overlooked. Avoid over-explaining Sarai's arrangement to young children; focus on the theme of God's sight and care.
application_for_children: God sees you, even when you feel alone or forgotten. You can call out to Him any time — He is always watching and He always cares.

verses.kjv: Now Sarai Abram's wife bare him no children: and she had an handmaid, an Egyptian, whose name was Hagar.
verses.little_bible: Abram's wife Sarai had no children. She had a helper from Egypt named Hagar. Sarai's heart was sad because she wanted a baby so much.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Abram's wife Sarai had no children. Her helper was Hagar.
verses.meaning: Sarai's childlessness was a source of deep sadness, yet God had not forgotten His promise to give Abram many descendants.
verses.prayer: God, when I feel sad about waiting, help me trust that You have not forgotten me.
verses.discussion_question: How do you think Sarai felt not having a baby when God had promised Abram many children?
verses.family_discussion: Has your family ever had to wait a long time for something? How did you feel while you waited?
verses.do_it_today: Draw a picture of something you are waiting and hoping for, and talk to God about it.
verses.illustration_prompt: A woman with a sad but hopeful expression sitting near a tent, with another woman standing nearby in ancient desert clothing.

verses.kjv: And Sarai said unto Abram, Behold now, the LORD hath restrained me from bearing: I pray thee, go in unto my maid; it may be that I may obtain children by her. And Abram hearkened to the voice of Sarai.
verses.little_bible: Sarai told Abram, 'God has kept me from having children. Please have a baby with my helper Hagar, so we can have a family through her.' Abram listened to Sarai.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Sarai asked Abram to have a baby with Hagar. Abram listened to Sarai.
verses.meaning: Sarai tried to solve her problem her own way instead of trusting God's timing. This kind of impatience can bring more trouble than it solves.
verses.prayer: Lord, help me wait for Your plan instead of rushing ahead on my own.
verses.discussion_question: Why do you think Sarai tried to find her own solution instead of waiting for God?
verses.family_discussion: Can you think of a time when trying to fix a problem quickly made things harder instead of easier?
verses.do_it_today: When something feels hard today, pause and say a short prayer before deciding what to do.
verses.illustration_prompt: A woman gesturing and speaking earnestly to a man inside a tent, both dressed in ancient robes.

verses.kjv: And Sarai Abram's wife took Hagar her maid the Egyptian, after Abram had dwelt ten years in the land of Canaan, and gave her to her husband Abram to be his wife.
verses.little_bible: After Abram and Sarai had lived in Canaan for ten years, Sarai gave Hagar to Abram as another wife. They hoped this would bring them the family God had promised.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: After ten years in Canaan, Sarai gave Hagar to Abram.
verses.meaning: Ten years of waiting had passed since God's promise. The couple's decision to act on their own plan shows how hard it can be to trust God when time seems to run out.
verses.prayer: God, help me trust Your timing even when I have waited a long time.
verses.discussion_question: Why do you think ten years felt like a very long time to wait for a promise?
verses.family_discussion: Talk about a time your family had to trust God even though it felt like a long time to wait.
verses.do_it_today: Make a 'waiting journal' — write or draw something you are trusting God for this week.
verses.illustration_prompt: A sandy desert landscape with a small encampment of tents under a bright sun, suggesting time has passed.

verses.kjv: And he went in unto Hagar, and she conceived: and when she saw that she had conceived, her mistress was despised in her eyes.
verses.little_bible: Hagar became pregnant with Abram's baby. But when Hagar knew she was expecting, she began to look down on Sarai. That made things very difficult between them.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Hagar had a baby growing inside her and she began to look down on Sarai.
verses.meaning: Hagar's attitude changed when she had something Sarai did not. Pride and unkindness can grow quickly when we forget to be grateful and humble.
verses.prayer: God, keep my heart from being proud. Help me be kind to everyone.
verses.discussion_question: Why is it wrong to look down on someone else even if we feel important?
verses.family_discussion: How can we treat others with respect, even when things are going well for us?
verses.do_it_today: Do one kind thing for someone in your family today without being asked.
verses.illustration_prompt: Two women in ancient robes — one standing tall with crossed arms, the other looking away sadly.

verses.kjv: And Sarai said unto Abram, My wrong be upon thee: I have given my maid into thy bosom; and when she saw that she had conceived, I was despised in her eyes: the LORD judge between me and thee.
verses.little_bible: Sarai was upset with Abram. She said, 'This is your fault! I gave you my helper and now she treats me badly. May the LORD decide who is right.' The choice Sarai made had brought a lot of sadness.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Sarai was upset and said to Abram, 'This is your fault! May God judge us.'
verses.meaning: When we make choices outside of God's plan, hurting and blaming others often follow. Sarai's plan had created pain for the whole family.
verses.prayer: Lord, when I have done something wrong, help me own it and not blame others.
verses.discussion_question: Why do people sometimes blame others when their own plan goes wrong?
verses.family_discussion: How can families deal with hurt feelings in a kind and honest way?
verses.do_it_today: If you have blamed someone unfairly this week, say sorry to them today.
verses.illustration_prompt: A woman pointing a finger at a man in a worried expression, inside a tent with soft desert light coming in.

verses.kjv: But Abram said unto Sarai, Behold, thy maid is in thy hand; do to her as it pleaseth thee. And when Sarai dealt hardly with her, she fled from her face.
verses.little_bible: Abram told Sarai to handle it however she liked. Sarai was very harsh to Hagar, so Hagar ran away into the desert. She was alone, afraid, and had nowhere to go.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Sarai was unkind to Hagar. Hagar ran away into the desert alone.
verses.meaning: Hagar was hurt and alone in the wilderness — but God had not forgotten her. Even when people treat us unfairly, God sees our pain.
verses.prayer: God, when I feel hurt and alone, remind me that You are close and You see me.
verses.discussion_question: How do you think Hagar felt running away into the desert all by herself?
verses.family_discussion: What should we do if we ever see someone being treated harshly or unfairly?
verses.do_it_today: Look for someone who seems left out or lonely today and say something kind to them.
verses.illustration_prompt: A young woman walking alone across a dusty desert, her head low, carrying a small bundle.

verses.kjv: And the angel of the LORD found her by a fountain of water in the wilderness, by the fountain in the way to Shur.
verses.little_bible: In the middle of the dry desert, there was a spring of water. The Angel of the LORD found Hagar sitting by that spring. God sent His messenger to find her.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: God's angel found Hagar by a spring of water in the desert.
verses.meaning: God actively seeks out people who are lost, hurting, or alone. Hagar did not have to find God — He came to find her.
verses.prayer: Thank You, God, for coming to find me when I feel lost.
verses.discussion_question: What does it tell us about God that He sent His angel to find Hagar in the desert?
verses.family_discussion: Has God ever sent someone to help you or your family at just the right moment?
verses.do_it_today: Be like God's angel today — go and find someone who needs a kind word or a friend.
verses.illustration_prompt: A glowing figure meeting a tired woman sitting beside a small spring in a vast sandy wilderness.

verses.kjv: And he said, Hagar, Sarai's maid, whence camest thou? and whither wilt thou go? And she said, I flee from the face of my mistress Sarai.
verses.little_bible: The Angel called Hagar by her name and asked where she had come from and where she was going. Hagar answered honestly: 'I am running away from my mistress Sarai.' God knew her name and her pain.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The angel called Hagar by name. She said, 'I am running away from Sarai.'
verses.meaning: God knows our name, our story, and where we are going even when we feel lost. He asks us questions not because He doesn't know, but because He wants us to talk to Him.
verses.prayer: God, You know my name and You know my heart. Help me talk to You honestly.
verses.discussion_question: How does it feel to know that God knows your name and your story?
verses.family_discussion: Why is it important to be honest with God about how we feel, even when we are scared or sad?
verses.do_it_today: Tell God one honest thing about how you are feeling right now — just like Hagar did.
verses.illustration_prompt: A bright angelic figure bending kindly toward a young woman sitting by water, speaking face to face.

verses.kjv: And the angel of the LORD said unto her, Return to thy mistress, and submit thyself under her hands.
verses.little_bible: The Angel told Hagar to go back to Sarai and to be humble. This was a hard instruction, but God had a plan — and Hagar's story was not over yet.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The angel said, 'Go back to Sarai and be humble.'
verses.meaning: Sometimes God asks us to do hard things that we don't fully understand yet. Trusting and obeying God even in hard situations is an act of great faith.
verses.prayer: God, help me obey You even when Your instructions seem hard.
verses.discussion_question: Why do you think God asked Hagar to go back instead of letting her run far away?
verses.family_discussion: Can you think of a time when doing the hard, right thing turned out to be worth it?
verses.do_it_today: If there is something hard you know is the right thing to do, ask God for courage and take one small step today.
verses.illustration_prompt: An angel gesturing gently back in the direction of distant tents, with Hagar listening with a thoughtful expression.

verses.kjv: And the angel of the LORD said unto her, I will multiply thy seed exceedingly, that it shall not be numbered for multitude.
verses.little_bible: The Angel gave Hagar a wonderful promise: 'Your children and grandchildren will become so many that no one could count them all!' God had a big future planned for Hagar's family.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The angel promised Hagar: 'Your family will grow very, very big!'
verses.meaning: God's care for Hagar was not only for the moment — He had a grand plan for her future. No person is too small or overlooked for God to have a purpose for.
verses.prayer: Thank You, God, that You have big plans for me too.
verses.discussion_question: What does God's big promise to Hagar tell us about how much He values her?
verses.family_discussion: How does it make you feel to know that God has plans and a purpose for each person in your family?
verses.do_it_today: Write your name on a piece of paper and next to it write: 'God has a plan for me!' Put it somewhere you will see it.
verses.illustration_prompt: The angel gesturing toward a vast starry sky while Hagar looks up with wonder.

verses.kjv: And the angel of the LORD said unto her, Behold, thou art with child, and shalt bear a son, and shalt call his name Ishmael; because the LORD hath heard thy affliction.
verses.little_bible: The Angel said, 'You are going to have a son. Name him Ishmael, because God has heard how much you are hurting.' Ishmael means 'God hears.' God had been listening the whole time.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The angel said, 'Your son is named Ishmael — God heard your pain.'
verses.meaning: The name Ishmael means 'God hears.' God hears the cries of hurting people — even when no one else seems to notice.
verses.prayer: God, thank You that You always hear me when I cry out to You.
verses.discussion_question: What does the name Ishmael tell us about what kind of God the LORD is?
verses.family_discussion: Share a time when you prayed and felt that God truly heard you.
verses.do_it_today: Say the name 'Ishmael' out loud and remember: God hears. Then pray about something on your heart today.
verses.illustration_prompt: A tender scene of an angel speaking to Hagar, one hand pointing to her baby bump, under a warm evening sky.

verses.kjv: And he will be a wild man; his hand will be against every man, and every man's hand against him; and he shall dwell in the presence of all his brethren.
verses.little_bible: The Angel said Ishmael would grow up to be fierce and free, like a wild animal. He would live near all his relatives but often in conflict with them. God was honest about what the future held.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The angel said Ishmael would be wild and free, living near his family.
verses.meaning: God described Ishmael's character honestly and fully. God always tells the truth about our lives and futures, even when it is complex.
verses.prayer: God, thank You that You are always truthful with me. Help me accept what You say.
verses.discussion_question: Why is it helpful that God tells us the truth about things, even hard truths?
verses.family_discussion: Why is it important for families to be honest with each other, even when the truth is hard?
verses.do_it_today: Practice telling the truth today — even about small things — because God is a God of truth.
verses.illustration_prompt: A boy running freely across a desert plain, wind in his hair, with tents visible in the far distance.

verses.kjv: And she called the name of the LORD that spake unto her, Thou God seest me: for she said, Have I also here looked after him that seeth me?
verses.little_bible: Hagar was so amazed that God had seen and spoken to her that she gave Him a special name: 'El Roi — the God who sees me.' She could not believe that the great God had noticed her.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Hagar named God 'El Roi' — the God who sees me!
verses.meaning: Hagar became the first person in the Bible to give God a name. El Roi means 'the God who sees.' No one is invisible to God — He sees every tear, every fear, every lonely moment.
verses.memory_phrase: You are the God who sees me
verses.prayer: God, You see me! You see everything about me. Thank You for never looking away.
verses.discussion_question: Why do you think Hagar was so amazed that God had seen her?
verses.family_discussion: How does it change the way you feel to know that God sees you — truly sees you — every moment of every day?
verses.do_it_today: Whenever you feel unseen or forgotten today, whisper 'El Roi — God sees me' as a prayer.
verses.illustration_prompt: A woman kneeling by a well in the desert, face lifted upward with wonder and tears of joy, hands open.

verses.kjv: Wherefore the well was called Beerlahairoi; behold, it is between Kadesh and Bered.
verses.little_bible: The spring where Hagar met God was given a special name: Beer-lahai-roi, which means 'The well of the Living One who sees me.' It stood as a reminder that God sees and cares for everyone who comes to Him.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The well was named 'The well of the Living One who sees me.'
verses.meaning: Places where people meet God can become reminders of His faithfulness. The name Beer-lahai-roi told everyone who passed by that God is alive and He sees people.
verses.memory_phrase: The well where God saw me
verses.prayer: Thank You, God, that You are the Living God who sees me every day.
verses.discussion_question: Why do you think people named special places after important things that happened there?
verses.family_discussion: Is there a place your family goes that reminds you of God's goodness or care?
verses.do_it_today: Pick a special spot in your home or yard and call it your 'prayer spot' — a place where you can talk to God.
verses.illustration_prompt: A stone well in the desert with a small carved name marker, surrounded by gentle sunlight and sparse green plants.

verses.kjv: And Hagar bare Abram a son: and Abram called his son's name, which Hagar bare, Ishmael.
verses.little_bible: Hagar had her baby — a boy! Abram named him Ishmael, just as the Angel of the LORD had said. God's word had come true exactly as He promised.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Hagar had a baby boy. Abram named him Ishmael.
verses.meaning: God's word always comes to pass. He had told Hagar her son's name before he was born, and it happened exactly that way.
verses.prayer: God, Your words always come true. Help me trust everything You say.
verses.discussion_question: What does it mean to you that Abram named the baby exactly what God said?
verses.family_discussion: Talk about one promise in the Bible your family is trusting God to keep.
verses.do_it_today: Find one promise from the Bible and read it together as a family today.
verses.illustration_prompt: A man gently holding a swaddled newborn baby, smiling down at him in warm lamplight inside a tent.

verses.kjv: And Abram was fourscore and six years old, when Hagar bare Ishmael to Abram.
verses.little_bible: Abram was 86 years old when Ishmael was born. God's story with Abram was still not finished — there was still so much more to come.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Abram was 86 years old when Ishmael was born.
verses.meaning: Even at 86, God was still working in Abram's life. God's story for His people does not end — it keeps unfolding in wonderful ways.
verses.prayer: God, thank You that Your plans for me never stop. Keep working in my life!
verses.discussion_question: What does it tell us about God that He was still working in Abram's life at 86 years old?
verses.family_discussion: How can your family keep trusting that God is still at work, even when things are changing or hard?
verses.do_it_today: As a family, thank God for one thing He has been doing in your lives this year.
verses.illustration_prompt: An elderly man with a white beard looking lovingly at a small baby wrapped in cloth, desert stars visible through the tent opening.
